pi_items.source: Matches task description explicitly naming Texas, California, and West Virginia.
Matches task description and export_result.sh which queries moz_bookmarks for 'grid analysis'.
Matches task description URL (https://www.eia.gov/state/) and export_result.sh query for '%eia.gov%'.
Matches task description instruction and export_result.sh checking this path.

Verified against live EIA data. TX Production (~27,105) > 15000, TX Price (~14.9) < 20.0, TX Rank (~1) <= 5. CA Production (~2,144) < 10000, CA Price (~33.6) > 20.0, CA Rank (~4) <= 15. WV Production (~8,144) < 10000, WV Price (~16.1) < 20.0, WV Rank (~18) <= 25.

privileged_info_summary: Verified that the task operates on live (non-synthetic) data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (eia.gov). The required target states are Texas, California, and West Virginia. The expected output file path is '/home/ga/Documents/energy_comparison.json', and the expected bookmark folder is 'Grid Analysis'. The loose data validation ranges claimed in the metadata (e.g., Texas production > 15,000 Trillion Btu, California price > 20.0 cents/kWh, West Virginia rank <= 25) were all empirically confirmed to be accurate descriptions of current real-world data from eia.gov.
